metadata.description: Comprehensive transmission and distribution technology database aligned with the Open Energy Ontology (OEO). Covers AC/DC electrical networks, gas/H2/CO2 pipelines, and district heating networks. Designed for PyPSA, Calliope, OSeMOSYS, and ADOPTNet0 energy system modeling.

notes.capex_normalization: For line/cable/pipeline technologies, capex_usd_per_kw is normalized over a representative corridor length (stated in instance_name). Primary engineering cost expression is also noted (USD/km or USD/MW-km).
notes.efficiency_percent: For electrical lines: (1 - total losses for stated corridor length) × 100. For pipelines: pipeline transport efficiency including compressor parasitic load over stated distance.
notes.ramping_rate: For electrical lines: limited in practice by protection relay speed (~999%/min effective). For pipelines: limited by compressor acceleration and line-packing (~0.5–5%/min).
notes.co2_factor: All transmission infrastructure has zero direct operational CO2 emissions. Upstream emissions from compressor fuel (gas pipelines) counted in fuel chain.

primary_sources: IEA World Energy Outlook 2023 – Grid Investment
IRENA Future of Solar PV 2019 – Grid Costs
CIGRE Technical Brochure TB 812 – HVDC Economics
European Commission Hydrogen Backbone Study 2021 (GIE/Hydrogen Europe)
Euroheat & Power District Heating Cost Study 2022

technologies.description: High-voltage alternating current (HVAC) overhead transmission lines on lattice steel towers. Operating at 220–400 kV (Europe) or 230–765 kV (North America). Dominant technology for bulk power transmission up to ~600 km. Reactive power compensation required at longer distances.

instances.instance_name: HVAC Overhead – 400 kV, 1000 MW, 200 km Corridor (Current, 2024)
instances.reference_source: ENTSO-E TYNDP 2022 Cost Data

instances.instance_name: HVAC Overhead – 400 kV, 1500 MW, 500 km Corridor + SVC (Current, 2024)
instances.reference_source: ENTSO-E TYNDP 2022; DIW Berlin HVAC Cost Analysis 2019

instances.instance_name: HVAC Overhead – 765 kV UHV, 3000 MW, 1000 km (Brazil/India-class, 2024)
instances.reference_source: IEA World Energy Outlook 2023 – Grid Investment

technologies.description: High-voltage direct current (HVDC) overhead transmission lines using VSC or LCC converter technology. Lower line losses than HVAC at distances > ~600 km. Enables asynchronous grid interconnection and precise power flow control. Converter stations are significant cost items.

instances.instance_name: HVDC Overhead – ±320 kV VSC, 1000 MW, 500 km (Current, 2024)
instances.reference_source: CIGRE Technical Brochure TB 812 – HVDC Economics 2021

instances.instance_name: HVDC Overhead – ±500 kV LCC, 3000 MW, 1500 km (Current, 2024)
instances.reference_source: ENTSO-E TYNDP 2022; IEA Grid Report 2023

instances.instance_name: HVDC Overhead – ±800 kV UHV-DC LCC, 6400 MW, 2000 km (China SGCC-class)
instances.reference_source: SGCC UHV-DC Project Reports (Zhundong–Wannan 2020)

technologies.description: High-voltage AC underground XLPE cables installed in direct-buried or ducted trenches. Used in dense urban areas, protected landscapes, and short sub-sea crossings where overhead lines are not feasible. Significantly higher cost than overhead lines; shorter practical lengths due to reactive power issues.

instances.instance_name: HVAC Underground – 132 kV, 200 MW, 30 km Urban Subterranean (Current, 2024)
instances.reference_source: ENTSO-E TYNDP 2022 Cost Data

instances.instance_name: HVAC Underground – 400 kV, 1000 MW, 50 km Transmission Route (Current, 2024)
instances.reference_source: CIGRE TB 812; IEA Grid Report 2023

instances.instance_name: HVAC Underground – 132 kV Advanced XLPE, 300 MW, 50 km (Future, 2035)
instances.reference_source: IEA World Energy Outlook 2023 – Grid Modernisation

technologies.description: HVDC cables installed on the seabed for international power interconnection and offshore wind grid connection. VSC technology dominates new projects. High installation costs (cable-laying vessels) but suitable for 50–1000+ km offshore routes.

instances.instance_name: HVDC Subsea – ±200 kV VSC, 700 MW, 100 km (Short Interconnector, 2024)
instances.reference_source: CIGRE TB 812 – HVDC Economics 2021

instances.instance_name: HVDC Subsea – ±320 kV VSC, 1400 MW, 400 km (Medium Interconnector, 2024)
instances.reference_source: ENTSO-E TYNDP 2022 (e.g., NordLink, NSL data)

instances.instance_name: HVDC Subsea – ±525 kV VSC, 2000 MW, 500 km Advanced (Future, 2035)
instances.reference_source: BloombergNEF Long-Term Energy Scenarios 2023 – Offshore Grid

technologies.description: Power transformers stepping voltage up or down between transmission and distribution levels. Core infrastructure at every substation. FACTS transformers (e.g., phase-shifting PSTs) provide additional power flow control.

instances.instance_name: Transformer – 100 MVA, 220/33 kV HV/MV (Substation, Current 2024)
instances.reference_source: IEC / IEEE Transformer Standards; ENTSO-E Asset Data 2021

instances.instance_name: Transformer – 400 MVA, 400/132 kV UHV/HV (Extra-Large Grid Node, 2024)
instances.reference_source: CIGRE WG A2.43 – Transformer Design Life 2019

instances.instance_name: Transformer – 300 MVA Phase-Shifting (PST/FACTS, Power Flow Control, 2024)
instances.reference_source: ENTSO-E Phase-Shifting Transformer Study 2020

technologies.description: High-pressure (50–100 bar) steel pipelines transporting natural gas over long distances. Compressor stations every 100–150 km maintain pressure. efficiency_percent reflects pipeline transport efficiency including compressor parasitic load. capex_usd_per_kw normalised over a stated corridor.

instances.instance_name: Natural Gas Pipeline – 1 GW equiv., DN800, 300 km (Regional, Current 2024)
instances.reference_source: IEA World Energy Outlook 2023 – Gas Infrastructure

instances.instance_name: Natural Gas Pipeline – 10 GW equiv., DN1200, 1500 km (Intercontinental, 2024)
instances.reference_source: IEA Gas Infrastructure - Nord Stream / TAP Reference Data 2022

instances.instance_name: Natural Gas Pipeline – 3 GW equiv., DN1000, 500 km H2-Blended (20% H2, 2030)
instances.reference_source: European Hydrogen Backbone Study (GIE/Hydrogen Europe) 2021

technologies.description: New-build or repurposed steel pipelines transporting pure hydrogen gas at 30–100 bar. Higher compressor energy requirements than CH4 (different molecular weight and viscosity). European Hydrogen Backbone initiative targets 4000 km of H2 pipelines by 2030 and 28,000 km by 2040.

instances.instance_name: H2 Pipeline – 1 GW equiv., Repurposed NG DN800, 300 km (Current, 2026)
instances.reference_source: European Hydrogen Backbone Study 2021 – Repurposed NG Cost

instances.instance_name: H2 Pipeline – 2 GW equiv., New-Build DN900, 500 km (Current, 2028)
instances.reference_source: IEA Global Hydrogen Review 2023 – H2 Pipeline Costs

instances.instance_name: H2 Pipeline – 5 GW equiv., DN1200, 2000 km Intercontinental (Future, 2035)
instances.reference_source: MENA-to-Europe Hydrogen Pipeline Study (IRENA/Hydrogen Europe 2022)

technologies.description: Carbon dioxide transport pipelines for CCS/CCU value chains, typically operating in supercritical dense-phase (above 73 bar) to minimise pipeline diameter. Connect capture sites to geological storage locations. efficiency_percent = pipeline transport efficiency (fraction of CO2 delivered). capex normalised per kW of associated capture input.

instances.instance_name: CO2 Pipeline – 2 MtCO2/yr, DN300, 100 km (Onshore, Current 2024)
instances.reference_source: IEA CCUS Transport Infrastructure 2022

instances.instance_name: CO2 Pipeline – 10 MtCO2/yr, DN600, 400 km (Shared Infrastructure, 2028)
instances.reference_source: Northern Lights / AKER Carbon Capture Project Cost Report 2022

instances.instance_name: CO2 Pipeline – 5 MtCO2/yr, DN400, 200 km Offshore to Storage (2030)
instances.reference_source: IEAGHG CO2 Offshore Transport and Storage Report 2020

technologies.description: Insulated pipe networks distributing hot water (3rd gen: 70–90 °C; 4th gen: 40–60 °C) from centralised heat sources to buildings. Efficiency depends on pipe insulation quality, supply temperature, and network length. Includes distribution pumps and heat substations.

instances.instance_name: District Heating – 50 MW 3rd Gen (Small Town, ~10 km Network, Current 2024)
instances.reference_source: Euroheat & Power District Heating Cost Study 2022

instances.instance_name: District Heating – 500 MW 3rd Gen (Large City, ~80 km Network, 2024)
instances.reference_source: Danish Energy Agency – Heat Supply Technology Data 2022

instances.instance_name: District Heating – 200 MW 4th Gen Low-Temp (40–60 °C, Renewable-Ready, 2035)
instances.reference_source: IEA Renewables Integration in District Heating 2023

technologies.description: Insulated pipe networks distributing chilled water (5–12 °C supply / 14–18 °C return) from centralised chillers to buildings for air conditioning. Common in dense urban districts, airports, and university campuses. Avoids per-building chillers; improves COP via large-scale efficiency gains. efficiency_percent reflects pipe heat-gain losses and pump parasitic consumption.

instances.instance_name: District Cooling – 30 MW Urban Network (3rd Gen, ~8 km, Current 2024)
instances.reference_source: IEA District Cooling – Technology and Policy Brief 2022; Euroheat & Power 2022

instances.instance_name: District Cooling – 200 MW Large City Network (~40 km, Current 2024)
instances.reference_source: Tabreed Annual Report 2023 (UAE district cooling operator); IEA 2022

instances.instance_name: District Cooling – 500 MW 4th Gen Smart Network (Seawater-Cooled, Future 2035)
instances.reference_source: IEA Renewables Integration in Cooling 2023; Stockholm Exergi ABB Report 2030 Vision

technologies.description: Road transport of compressed gaseous hydrogen (200–700 bar) in tube-trailer bundles or cryogenic liquid tankers. Bridging technology before pipeline infrastructure is deployed. Capacity per trip: 200–1000 kg H2 (gaseous tube trailer) or 3000–5000 kg (liquid tanker). efficiency_percent = delivered H2 fraction (accounting for ullage and boil-off). capex_usd_per_kw normalised per kW of refuelling-station equivalent throughput.

instances.instance_name: H2 Tube Trailer – 200 bar, ~320 kg/trip, Gaseous (Current, 2024)
instances.reference_source: NREL Hydrogen Delivery Infrastructure Analysis 2023; US DOE H2A Model

instances.instance_name: H2 Tube Trailer – 500 bar, ~900 kg/trip, High-Pressure Gaseous (Current, 2024)
instances.reference_source: IEA Global Hydrogen Review 2023 – Road Transport Costs

instances.instance_name: H2 Liquid Tanker – Cryogenic LH2, ~4000 kg/trip (Future, 2030)
instances.reference_source: Hydrogen Council – Hydrogen Insights 2022; IEA 2023

technologies.description: Low-to-medium pressure pipelines (1–16 bar) transporting raw or upgraded biogas (biomethane) from production sites (digesters, landfills) to end users or grid injection points. Biogas contains 50–70% CH4 + CO2; biomethane is upgraded to >95% CH4. Compressor stations at 20–60 km intervals. efficiency_percent reflects pipeline transport efficiency including compressor parasitic load.

instances.instance_name: Biogas Pipeline – Raw Biogas, 10 MW equiv., DN 200, 20 km Regional (Current, 2024)
instances.reference_source: IEA Outlook for Biogas and Biomethane 2020; JRC Biomethane Potential Europe 2022

instances.instance_name: Biomethane Pipeline – Upgraded Grid-Injection, 100 MW equiv., DN 400, 100 km (2026)
instances.reference_source: European Biogas Association Statistical Report 2023; IEA 2022

instances.instance_name: Biomethane Pipeline – National Grid Integration, 500 MW equiv. (Future, 2035)
instances.reference_source: European Commission REPowerEU – Biomethane 35 Bcm Target 2022; IEA 2023

technologies.description: Road transport of solid biomass (wood chips, pellets, agricultural residues, straw) in tipper trucks or walking-floor trailers. Dominant mode for distances < 200 km. Payload: 20–30 t dry matter per truck. efficiency_percent ≈ delivered energy fraction (accounting for fuel combustion in transport). capex_usd_per_kw normalised per average throughput kW.

instances.instance_name: Biomass Truck – Wood Chips Short Haul, <50 km (Current, 2024)
instances.reference_source: IRENA Bioenergy Supply Chains and Costs 2019; IEA Bioenergy Task 40 2021

instances.instance_name: Biomass Truck – Pellets Medium Haul, ~200 km (Current, 2024)
instances.reference_source: European Biomass Industry Association Logistics Report 2022

instances.instance_name: Biomass Truck – Electric/H2 Fuel-Cell HGV, 150 km (Future, 2035)
instances.reference_source: IEA Trucking Clean Transitions 2023; IRENA 2023

technologies.description: Rail freight transport of solid biomass (wood pellets, chips, straw bales) in open wagons or covered hopper cars. More energy-efficient than trucking at distances > 200 km. Payload: 1500–3000 t dry matter per train. Suitable for national and intercontinental biomass supply chains (e.g. pellet export from North America to EU). efficiency_percent ≈ energy delivered / energy content assuming diesel/electric traction losses.

instances.instance_name: Biomass Rail – Pellets National, ~500 km, Diesel Traction (Current, 2024)
instances.reference_source: IEA Bioenergy Task 40 – Biomass Supply Chain Costs 2021

instances.instance_name: Biomass Rail – Pellets International, ~2000 km, Mixed Traction (Current, 2024)
instances.reference_source: IRENA Bioenergy Supply Chains 2019; Drax Pellet Supply Chain Report 2022

instances.instance_name: Biomass Rail – Electrified / H2 Rail, 1000 km (Future, 2035)
instances.reference_source: European Commission Green Rail Freight Initiative 2023

technologies.description: High-pressure (50–100 bar) pipelines transporting crude oil or refined petroleum products (diesel, kerosene, naphtha). Pump stations every 30–100 km maintain flow. Among the lowest-cost transport modes per unit energy. efficiency_percent reflects viscous-flow pump parasitic load over stated corridor.

instances.instance_name: Oil Pipeline – Crude Oil, 500 MW equiv., DN 500, 200 km (Current, 2024)
instances.reference_source: IEA Oil 2023 – Midstream Infrastructure; World Bank Petroleum Transport Costs 2022

instances.instance_name: Oil Pipeline – Petroleum Products, 2000 MW equiv., DN 800, 500 km (Current, 2024)
instances.reference_source: IEA World Energy Outlook 2023 – Oil Infrastructure

instances.instance_name: Oil Pipeline – Repurposed for e-Fuels / SAF, 1000 MW equiv. (Future, 2035)
instances.reference_source: IEA Advanced Biofuels and Synthetic Fuels 2022

technologies.description: Road transport of liquid petroleum products (diesel, petrol, kerosene, LPG, biodiesel, HVO) in pressurised or ambient-temperature tank trailers. Payload: 30,000–40,000 litres (≈ 270–380 GJ per vehicle). Used for last-mile distribution and in areas without pipeline access. efficiency_percent ≈ 1.0 (negligible energy loss; emissions from traction fuel counted separately).

instances.instance_name: Fuel Tanker – Diesel/Petrol, 35,000 L, Short Haul <200 km (Current, 2024)
instances.reference_source: IEA Oil 2023; OECD International Transport Forum Freight Statistics 2022

instances.instance_name: Fuel Tanker – HVO / Biodiesel, 35,000 L (Current, 2024)
instances.reference_source: IRENA Advanced Biofuels Costs 2022

instances.instance_name: Fuel Tanker – Electric Truck, e-Fuel / SAF, 250 km Range (Future, 2035)
instances.reference_source: IEA Trucking Clean Transitions 2023; IRENA 2023

technologies.description: Large-diameter pressurised pipelines (ductile iron, steel, prestressed concrete) transmitting potable or process water from sources (reservoirs, desalination plants) to distribution networks. Pump stations overcome elevation and friction losses. efficiency_percent = water delivered / water abstracted (accounting for pump energy parasitics and leakage). capex_usd_per_kw normalised per kW of hydraulic power throughput.

instances.instance_name: Water Pipeline – Municipal Potable, DN 600, 30 km (Current, 2024)
instances.reference_source: World Bank Water Infrastructure Costing 2022; IWA Best Practices 2023

instances.instance_name: Water Pipeline – Bulk Transfer, DN 1200, 200 km (Interbasin, Current 2024)
instances.reference_source: IEA Water-Energy Nexus 2023; OECD Water Infrastructure Investment 2022

instances.instance_name: Water Pipeline – Smart Leak-Detection, DN 800, 100 km (Future, 2035)
instances.reference_source: IEA Water-Energy Nexus Report 2023; EU Water Resilience Strategy 2023

technologies.description: Pressurised insulated pipelines transporting industrial or district steam (100–250 °C, 3–25 bar gauge) from centralised boilers or CHP plants to industrial consumers, hospitals, or dense urban blocks. Higher temperature than hot-water district heating; used where process heat quality is required. efficiency_percent reflects heat losses in distribution at operating temperature. Condensate return credited.

instances.instance_name: Steam Network – Industrial Park, 50 MW, 10 bar, 5 km Loop (Current, 2024)
instances.reference_source: Euroheat & Power District Heating Cost Study 2022; IEA Industrial Steam 2023

instances.instance_name: Steam Network – Urban District, 200 MW, 6 bar, 20 km (Current, 2024)
instances.reference_source: Danish Energy Agency – Heat Supply Technology Data 2022

instances.instance_name: Steam Network – Low-Pressure 3 bar Pre-Insulated, 100 MW (Future, 2035)
instances.reference_source: IEA Heating and Cooling from Renewables 2023
